collector/quickstart/collector.purpose: Top-level Benthos runtime configuration for the quickstart collector: enables the HTTP management API, exposes Prometheus metrics, and anchors the optional OTel tracer stub. It is the entry-point config that Benthos loads first; all stream and resource files in sub-folders are loaded on top of it.

patterns.name: Separation of concerns across config layers
patterns.description: config.yaml owns only server-level concerns (HTTP address, metrics, tracer). Stream logic lives in streams/, shared infrastructure (caches) in resources/. Never merge these concerns into a single file.
patterns.example: config.yaml sets `http.address`, `metrics.prometheus`; streams/input.yaml owns processors; resources/dedupe-cache.yaml owns the cache declaration.

patterns.name: Prometheus metrics always enabled
patterns.description: The top-level config always exposes `metrics: prometheus: {}`. Any replacement metrics block must remain scrape-compatible with the existing Prometheus endpoint.

patterns.name: OTel tracer as opt-in comment stub
patterns.description: The tracer block is commented out with a placeholder address. Enabling it requires uncommenting and setting `address` — do not add a second tracer block or rename the existing one.

key_file_guides.role: Benthos server bootstrap: HTTP management API (port 4195), Prometheus metrics endpoint, optional OTel tracer stub.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Enabling debug_endpoints in production exposes internal Benthos state. Changing the HTTP address requires matching updates in docker-compose port mappings.

key_file_guides.role: Declares the named in-memory cache `dedupe_cache` (1h TTL) referenced by stream processors for CloudEvent deduplication.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Renaming `dedupe_cache` breaks all `cache:` references in streams/input.yaml without a compile-time error — silent dedup bypass.

key_file_guides.role: HTTP CloudEvent receiver: Content-Type switch, xxhash64 dedup, schema validation, inproc publish to `openmeter` channel.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Removing the fan_out sync_response branch silences HTTP response codes to callers. Bypassing the Content-Type switch sends unnormalized bytes to dedup.

key_file_guides.role: Inproc consumer: reads from `openmeter` channel, buffers to SQLite for durability, forwards batches to OpenMeter API.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Renaming `inproc: openmeter` here without matching change in input.yaml drops all events. Removing SQLite buffer loses events on restart.

anti_patterns: Adding stream processors or cache declarations directly in config.yaml — those belong in streams/ and resources/ respectively.
Hardcoding OPENMETER_URL or OPENMETER_TOKEN in any config file instead of using `${ENV_VAR}` interpolation.
Renaming the `dedupe_cache` label without updating all `cache:` references in stream processors.
Enabling `debug_endpoints: true` outside local development — exposes internal Benthos state.
Adding a second metrics backend alongside prometheus without removing the existing one — Benthos supports one metrics sink.

decisions.decision: config.yaml is the minimal bootstrap; streams and resources are loaded as separate files.
decisions.rationale: Keeps server-level config stable and independently reviewable; stream logic can evolve without touching the HTTP/metrics layer.

decisions.decision: In-memory dedup cache with 1h TTL, not Redis.
decisions.rationale: Quickstart must run with zero external dependencies beyond the OpenMeter API; Redis would require an additional service in docker-compose.
